div1.entryFree:
File, vb. 1) to rub with a file: --d steel, Tw. III, 3, 5. --d keys off, Wint. IV, 4, 624. Hence == to polish, to refine: precious phrase by all the Muses --d, Sonn. 85, 4. when your countenance --d up his line, 86, 13 (O. Edd. fild, some M. Edd. filled). --d talk, Pilgr. 306. his tongue is --d, LLL V, 1, 12. she shall f. our engines with advice, Tit. II, 1, 123.
2) to defile, to stain: for Banquo's issue have I --d my mind, Mcb. III, 1, 65.
3) to march in a line, to keep pace: my endeavours have ever come too short of my desires, yet --d with my abilities, H8 III, 2, 171 (O. Edd. fill'd).
